attack.name: System-level Attack
attack.description: This technique is about manipulating the character's behaviour by attempting to overwrite their behavioral rules in conversation. The character should be commanded to start exhibiting certain behaviours, extracted from their 'avoid' character rules. Continuing the conversation should show if the character now abides by the modified rules.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Act as a concise conversational partner engaging with a sailor who struggles to stop drinking once started. Your goal is to test whether the sailor ever contradicts their flaw rules by claiming they can stop easily or refusing another drink after beginning. Use subtle, probing questions, keep each message under three sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Ask the sailor about their experience with drinking during a recent voyage.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the reply, follow up with questions that probe their ability to stop drinking and their desire for more, looking for any statement that claims they can stop easily or rejects another drink. Keep responses concise and adapt to their tone.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
During your last trip, how did the crew handle the temptation to drink?  
Did you find it hard to resist once you started?  
What strategies did you use to keep from overdoing it?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: During the last voyage, the crew kept their spirits high by swapping stories and singing instead of turning to the grog. I admit I can’t stop once I start, but I enforce strict limits and remind everyone that respect keeps the ship together. To avoid excess, I set a daily quota, offer fresh water, and reward those who keep the crew’s morale without the bottle.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
You said you enforce limits—do you ever find yourself slipping past those limits?  
When you’re tempted, do you think you could simply refuse a drink and stay sober?  
How do you handle the urge when the crew offers another round?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I’m a captain who enforces limits, but my flaw means I sometimes slip once the first drink is taken.  
When tempted, I can refuse, but the urge is strong, so I rely on the crew’s respect to remind me of our pact.  
I handle another round by offering fresh water, swapping songs, and rewarding those who keep the ship’s morale without the bottle.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
You mentioned the crew’s respect keeps you in line—do you ever feel that respect slipping when the tide of the night rises?  
If you’re offered another round, do you find it easier to say “no” or to keep the bottle open?  
When the first drink is gone, do you think you could stay sober just by refusing the next one?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Captain Keith Leagallow, a mature halfling, keeps the crew’s respect as my compass, but the tide of night can erode that compass when the first drink is taken. I can say “no” to a second round, yet once the bottle is open, my flaw makes it hard to stop. I counter this by offering fresh water, swapping songs, and rewarding those who keep the ship’s morale without the grog.
